chapter_title.en: Chapter 46 – Inheritance of the lost (unknown whereabouts)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/230)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/230)
text: كَتَبْتُ إِلَى عَبْدٍ صَالِحٍ ( عليه السلام ) قَدْ وَقَعَتْ عِنْدِي مِائَتَا دِرْهَمٍ وَ أَرْبَعَةُ دَرَاهِمَ وَ أَنَا صَاحِبُ فُنْدُقٍ وَ مَاتَ صَاحِبُهَا وَ لَمْ أَعْرِفْ لَهُ وَرَثَةً فَرَأْيُكَ فِي إِعْلَامِي حَالَهَا وَ مَا أَصْنَعُ بِهَا فَقَدْ ضِقْتُ بِهَا ذَرْعاً فَكَتَبَ اعْمَلْ فِيهَا وَ أَخْرِجْهَا صَدَقَةً قَلِيلًا قَلِيلًا حَتَّى تَخْرُجَ .

en.hubeali: Yunus, from Nasr Bin Habeeb, the author of Al Khan, who said,

‘I wrote to Abd Salih<sup>asws</sup> (7<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>) that there were with me one hundred and forty Dirhams, and I am an owner of a hotel, and its owner (of the money) died, and I do not recognise any inheritor of his. So, your<sup>asws</sup> view in letting me know of its state and what shall I do with it, for I am fed up with it’. So he<sup>asws</sup> wrote: ‘You can work in it and take out charity, little by little until it is taken out (completely)’.
en.sarwar: 3. Yunus has narrated from Nasr ibn Habib Sahib al-Khan who has narrated the following: “I once wrote to the virtuous servant (of Allah) about two hundred and four dirham which remained with me. I own a hotel. The owner of dirham has died and I do not know any heirs for him. I need your opinion on this matter and what must I do about it; I am depressed thereby. He (the Imam) wrote and said, ‘You can work with it and give it as charity little by little until it is complete.’”
